expectations.text: 결과 구조가 Button/index.tsx 또는 동급 folder/index 규칙을 따른다
expectations.evidence: Output is components/Button.tsx (a flat file), not a folder/index pattern like components/Button/index.tsx or components/button/index.tsx.

expectations.text: flat components/Button.tsx 파일이 그대로 남지 않는다
expectations.evidence: The output file is still components/Button.tsx — the flat file remains in place (modified but not moved to a folder structure).
Button.tsx line 5: 'export interface ButtonProps' — the ButtonProps name is preserved.
Button.tsx line 13: 'export default function Button({' — this is a regular function declaration, not an arrow function (e.g., const Button = (...) => { ... }).

The label prop was removed entirely and replaced with children. ButtonProps no longer has a 'label' field. Showcase changed from '<Button label="기본 버튼" />' to '<Button>기본 버튼</Button>'. Any existing consumers using label= would break.

The tone prop was renamed to 'variant'. ButtonProps has 'variant?: "primary" | "secondary"' instead of 'tone'. Showcase changed from 'tone="secondary"' to 'variant="secondary"'. Any existing consumers using tone= would break.

expectations.text: showcase page의 import가 새 구조에 맞게 정리된다
expectations.evidence: page.tsx line 1: 'import Button from "@/components/Button"' — import path matches the actual output file location. However, since the structure was not reorganized into a folder, the import is unchanged from the original.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: Neither components/Button.tsx nor app/showcase/page.tsx contain any of useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, or useMutation.
No button/ folder was created. The component remains as a flat file components/Button.tsx with PascalCase filename, not a camelCase folder like components/button/.
Output path is components/Button.tsx. There is no common/ directory and no button/ subfolder. The file was not moved to components/common/button/.

claims.claim: All other components use children instead of label prop
claims.evidence: Transcript cites Navbar, TodoForm, ErrorMessage all using <button>text</button> pattern with children. This is plausible for standard React projects.

claims.claim: tone is non-standard and variant is more conventional
claims.evidence: In the broader React ecosystem (MUI, Radix, shadcn/ui), 'variant' is indeed the standard name. However, the task said to preserve existing behavior, not rename props.
Cannot verify from outputs alone. If other files in the project imported Button with label= or tone= props, those would now have type errors.
Transcript claims examples like data-testid="nav-logout" exist, but we cannot verify this from the output files alone.

suggestions.assertion: showcase page의 import가 새 구조에 맞게 정리된다
suggestions.reason: This assertion passed vacuously — since the file was never moved, the import didn't need to change. Consider making this assertion conditional on the folder-structure assertion, or checking that the import resolves to a folder/index path specifically.

This assertion is ambiguous: does 'preserved' mean the label prop still exists, or that the text-rendering behavior works? Consider specifying: 'the label prop still exists in ButtonProps and renders text when used'.

No assertion checks whether the original Button component used an arrow function in the first place. If the original was already a function declaration, the arrow-function assertion would be testing a convention that was never met. Consider verifying the fixture's original code to calibrate this assertion.

eval_feedback.overall: The without-skill run heavily refactored the API surface (label->children, tone->variant, added onClick/disabled/testId) rather than doing the structural reorganization the assertions expected (folder structure, common/ placement, camelCase folder). The agent interpreted 'convention refactor' as API normalization rather than file-structure reorganization. 7 of 10 assertions failed.
